title: eryngium eriophorum var. typicum
intro: Eryngium eriophorum var. typicum, also known as White Sea Blush or White Sea Eringium, is a plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other compounds. The health benefits of Eryngium eriophorum var. typicum include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
